title: Ranunculus ostrobottnicus
intro: Ranunculus ostrobottnicus, also known as Northern Water Buttercup, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its shiny, yellow flowers and aquatic growth habits. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The bioactive constituents of Ranunculus ostrobottnicus include ranunculin, a toxic compound with potential anti-cancer properties, as well as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other secondary metabolites with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wound healing, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Ranunculus ostrobottnicus is a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. It belongs to the genus Ranunculus and is native to Europe. The plant is further classified as a member of the Ranunculeae tribe within the Ranunculaceae family. Ranunculus ostrobottnicus is also classified as a species of buttercup. Its classification is part of the larger system of plant taxonomy.

The plant is also believed to have diuretic and expectorant effects, which may help with respiratory problems and urinary tract issues. side_effects: Ranunculus ostrobottnicus is known to be highly toxic due to its alkaloids and glycosides content. Ingestion of the plant can cause severe gastrointestinal symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. The toxic compounds in Ranunculus ostrobottnicus can also lead to kidney damage and other systemic effects if ingested in large quantities. If the plant's sap comes into contact with the skin, it can cause irritation, redness, and blistering. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can have serious health consequences, including organ failure in severe cases.
